name: Chapatis
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Atta or whole wheat flour (sifted)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-sized bowl, mix together the salt and the sifted flour. Make a well in the middle and add just less than 1 cup warm water. Mix with your hand or spoon until you can gather it into a dough. Adjust hydration by adding a little more water or flour if needed.
2. Turn out onto a lightly floured bread board and knead for 8 to 10 minutes.
3. Cover with a damp cloth or plastic wrap and let stand for 30 minutes, up to 2 hours. Longer resting times yield more digestible breads.
4. Divide the dough into 8 pieces. Flatten each piece with lightly floured fingers.
5. Roll each piece until it is about 8 inches in diameter using a rolling pin. Avoid stacking rolled-out pieces; if needed, roll out only a few at a time and cook them while you finish the rest.
6. Heat a cast iron griddle or skillet over medium-high heat.
7. Place a chapati on the griddle, top side down first. Cook for 10 seconds, then gently flip to the other side. Cook until small bubbles form (about one minute).
8. Flip back and cook another minute. The bread should start to puff up.
9. Use a cloth or paper towel to press down on large bubbles to encourage them to spread wider. If burning occurs before the bread puffs, carefully move it with the towel across the skillet.
10. When ready, remove the chapati and wrap in a clean towel. Repeat for remaining breads.
Source: Bakers' Dozen, Alford and Duguid, TVFN (MM Waldine Van Geffen)
